label: experimental/frontier
rq: RQ33: Metadata-only Mode S detector (runtime + duration + segment count, NO content)
method: reanalysis only (no Whisper / no ASR run); 10 metadata-only features extracted per window. Per-feature two-sided calibration at >= 90% specificity on the 40 non-hallucinated tracks. Combined detector = numpy-only L2 logistic regression on all 10 features with leave-one-out cross-validation (Mode S label); threshold calibrated at 90% specificity on the 40 non-hallucinated out-of-fold probabilities. Ensemble = metadata LR flag OR lang-id entropy (threshold 0.409). Permutation test: 1000 permutations, seed=42, two-sided, +1 smoothing.
mode_s_definition: hallucinated AND lang_id_entropy < 0.409 AND length_ratio < 2.0 AND cr < 2.4 (escapes every surface detector; the RQ16 corrected-router residual)

feature_notes.runtime_ratio: sep_runtime / mix_runtime (RQ22 partial signal: caught 1/2 Mode S)
feature_notes.sep_total_chars: total character count of the separated transcript
feature_notes.mix_total_chars: total character count of the mixed transcript
feature_notes.num_speakers: number of speakers in the reference
feature_notes.num_active_speakers_sep: number of non-empty speaker segments in separated output (= RQ22 effective_speaker_count)
feature_notes.avg_speaker_length_sep: mean characters per non-empty speaker segment
feature_notes.length_entropy_speakers: Shannon entropy of per-speaker lengths (= RQ22 per_speaker_length_entropy)

mode_s_context.rq22_runtime_ratio_partial_signal: RQ22 found sep_to_mix_runtime_ratio (= runtime_ratio here) caught 1 of 2 Mode S (window 22, ratio 7.05) but missed window 30 (ratio 0.99). This study tests whether bundling all 10 metadata features into a logistic regression with LOO-CV catches both.
mode_s_context.mode_s_metadata_heterogeneity: Window 22 (runtime_ratio 7.05, num_speakers 2, 1 active speaker) and window 30 (runtime_ratio 0.99, num_speakers 1, 1 active speaker) have very different metadata profiles. LOO-CV must generalise from one to the other, which is the binding constraint.

thresholds.lr_l2_rationale: smallest L2 at which all LOO fold probabilities are non-saturated (in (0.01, 0.99)); a numerical-stability criterion for the 1:76 imbalance in each LOO fold, NOT a Mode-S-performance criterion. See l2_sensitivity_analysis.

per_feature_detectors.direction_meaning: flag if score >= threshold (high score = hallucination)

permutation_test.note: Two-sided permutation test: fraction of permutations with |stat| >= |observed|. With n_mode_s=2 the number of distinct labelings is C(77,2)=2926; p-value resolution is bounded and reported with +1 smoothing.

per_feature_detectors.direction_meaning: flag if score <= threshold (low score = hallucination)

permutation_test.note: Two-sided permutation test: fraction of permutations with |stat| >= |observed|. With n_mode_s=2 the number of distinct labelings is C(77,2)=2926; p-value resolution is bounded and reported with +1 smoothing.

best_single_feature_detector.selection_rule: lowest permutation p-value (most distinct Mode S profile); tiebreak highest Mode S sensitivity at 90% specificity

combined_metadata_lr.rule: numpy-only L2 logistic regression on all 10 metadata features; Mode S label; LOO-CV out-of-fold probabilities; flag if prob >= threshold

l2_sensitivity_analysis.note: LOO-CV Mode S sensitivity as a function of L2. With n=2 Mode S, L2 cannot be tuned by cross-validation; this grid exposes the fragility directly. 1 of 9 L2 values achieve 100% Mode S sensitivity. 'saturated' = at least one OOF probability in [0, 0.01] or [0.99, 1.0] (numerical pathology, not signal).

lang_id_entropy_alone_reference.note: RQ13 lang-id entropy detector. By definition Mode S escapes it (lang-id < 0.409), so lang-id alone misses both Mode S tracks.

H33a.statement: metadata-only detector catches both Mode S windows at 90% specificity (sensitivity = 100%)
H33a.detector: combined metadata LR (LOO-CV) at default L2

l2_robustness.interpretation: Only 1 of 9 L2 values achieve 100% Mode S sensitivity; the rest achieve 50%. The 100% result is therefore FRAGILE to the L2 hyperparameter, which cannot be tuned by cross-validation with n=2 Mode S.

H33a.verdict_qualifier: SUPPORTED at the default (minimum-stable) L2, but FRAGILE: only 1/9 L2 values achieve 100% Mode S sensitivity. Not robustly deployable.
H33a.reason: LOO-CV metadata LR catches 100% of Mode S at 100% specificity (threshold 0.1101) at the default L2=50.0. Window-22 prob=0.1101, window-30 prob=0.1104, top clean prob=0.0552. L2 sensitivity: 1/9 L2 values achieve 100% Mode S sensitivity (the rest 50%); the result is fragile because n=2 Mode S precludes L2 tuning by cross-validation.

H33b.statement: combined metadata + lang-id detector achieves > 95% sensitivity on the 37 AISHELL-4 hallucinated tracks

l2_robustness.interpretation: 9 of 9 L2 values yield ensemble (metadata OR lang-id) sensitivity > 95% on the 37 hallucinated. The ensemble reaches > 95% whenever the metadata LR catches >= 1 Mode S (lang-id already catches 35/37 diverse hallucinations), which happens at all L2 values tested. H33b is therefore robust on the sensitivity criterion; specificity is only good at non-saturated L2 (see l2 grid).

H33b.reason: Ensemble sensitivity is 100.0% (37/37) at 92.5% specificity; lang-id alone is 94.6% (35/37). Metadata LR catches 100% of Mode S, so the ensemble adds 2 Mode S track(s) over lang-id alone. Across L2, 9/9 values yield ensemble sensitivity > 95% (robust on sensitivity; specificity varies — good only at non-saturated L2).

H33c.statement: metadata features have a distinct Mode S profile (permutation p < 0.05 for > 50% of features)
H33c.reason: 2 of 10 features have perm p < 0.05 (best: avg_speaker_length_sep p=0.0030). Mode S's metadata profile is dominated by window 22's extreme runtime_ratio (7.05) and window 30's clean-like profile (runtime_ratio 0.99); averaging the two washes out the signal.
